title: Acetosa ellenbeckii
taxonomy: Acetosa ellenbeckii is classified within the family Amaranthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the amaranth family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Acetosa ellenbeckii falls under the subfamily Amaranthoideae. The genus Acetosa is closely related to the genus Rumex, which contains the sorrel plants. Within the genus Acetosa, Acetosa ellenbeckii is one of the species that is found in various parts of the world. The classification of Acetosa ellenbeckii provides insights into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.

Additionally, Acetosa ellenbeckii has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its ability to regulate bowel movements.
